pages.title: Current version
pages.text: Currently stable: $(l)入$(), $(l)劫$(), $(l)印$(), $(l)泉$(), $(l)墨$(), $(l)山$(), and $(l)裂$(). 入 handles supplies and brush momentum. 劫 handles stave breakthrough. 印, 泉, 墨, 山, and 裂 form the tactical base of the second wave.$(br2)Ink fields can drop ink cores; 入 itself does not.

pages.title: What ink cores are
pages.text: Ink cores are the long-term reward material for later second-wave systems: dungeons, ritual draws, and advanced progression.$(br2)They do not appear from ordinary body glyphs. They come from ink fields now, and later from dedicated challenges. 劫 is mainly for upgrading staves, not farming ink cores.

pages.title: Ritual draws later
pages.text: The planned draw system will be a world ritual: place an altar, socket ink cores, write the ritual glyph, then let the circle animation reveal the reward.$(br2)For now, store ink cores. The long-term spending loop is not open yet.
